title: phlomoides desertorum
intro: Phlomoides desertorum, also known as Desert Fleabane, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to the Western United States and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's leaves and flowers contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. Phlomoides desertorum can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and optimal dosing.
